heroHeadline: Trump Cancels Witkoff–Kushner Pakistan Trip; Iran Nuclear Round 2 Collapses and Lebanon Ceasefire Under Acute Strain
heroSubtitle: On April 26, Trump abruptly cancelled the planned Pakistan mission of Special Envoy Witkoff and Jared Kushner for a second round of Iran nuclear talks, calling Tehran's offer 'not enough' — both Islamabad rounds have now failed with the 20-year vs. 5-year enrichment gap unresolved. Simultaneously, Israel issued forced evacuation orders for seven southern Lebanon towns and continued airstrikes despite the 3-week ceasefire extension (granted April 23), putting the Lebanon–Israel framework under acute strain. Iranian FM Araghchi pressed on with a regional diplomatic tour (Oman, Gulf states), preserving back-channel architecture. The UN Security Council called on April 27 for consolidation of the Gaza Phase 2 ceasefire as Hamas rejected the US disarmament clause. 10 ceasefire frameworks nominally in force globally, down from 13 in January 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN, African Union, IGAD, Crisis Group, USIP, PA-X Database, and Tier 1–3 sources across all media poles.
